title: Peppery Dense German Pork Stew
description: A hearty and comforting German stew featuring tender pork, aromatic spices, and the unique flavor of khus khus, perfect for a cold winter's night. This dish is carefully prepared to be nut-free-friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diners.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent and starting to caramelize, about 8 minutes.
Add the pork cubes and cook until they're browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Grind the black peppercorns and mix with khus khus, then add this mixture to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add the beef broth, thyme, and salt. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1.5 hours, or until the pork and vegetables are tender.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
